title: Buttery Wholesome Spanish Tarta Santiago
description: A classic Spanish dessert featuring a buttery crust and a rich mascarpone filling, perfect for kids and adults alike.
instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Cut out a circle of dough large enough to fit a 9-inch (23 cm) tart pan.
Place the dough into the tart pan, pressing it into the corners.
Prick the bottom of the pastry with a fork to prevent it from bubbling up during baking.
Line the pastry with parchment paper and fill with pie weights or dried beans.
Bake the pastry for 15 minutes, then remove the parchment paper and pie weights or beans.
In a medium bowl, whip the mascarpone cheese until smooth.
Add the granulated sugar, unsalted butter, and lardons to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Pour the mascarpone mixture into the baked pastry shell.
Sprinkle the all-purpose flour and ground cinnamon over the top of the mascarpone mixture.
Bake the tart for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is set.
Allow the tart to cool completely before serving.
